categories.title: Offenses Involving the Operation of a Vehicle
Offenses Involving the Well-Being of Wildlife

modifiers.label: Protected Public Servant Victim
modifiers.description: Applies when the victim is acting within a protected public service role during the incident.
sourceTitles: Battery on a Public Servant or Peace Officer
Murder of a Public Servant or Peace Officer
Unlawful Imprisonment of a Public Servant or Peace Officer.

modifiers.label: Substantial Cooperation
modifiers.description: Applies when the defendant materially assists officers or investigators after the offense.

modifiers.label: No Lasting Injury
modifiers.description: Applies when the conduct did not cause lasting physical injury or medical complications.

modifiers.label: Weapon or Dangerous Instrument Used
modifiers.description: Applies when a weapon or dangerous instrument materially elevated the risk of harm.

modifiers.label: Multiple Victims
modifiers.description: Applies when the same criminal conduct involves more than one distinct victim.

modifiers.label: Aid Rendered After Incident
modifiers.description: Applies when the defendant took meaningful steps to summon or provide aid after the incident.

modifiers.label: Restitution Made
modifiers.description: Applies when the victim is reimbursed or property is returned before disposition.

modifiers.label: Organized Criminal Activity
modifiers.description: Applies when the offense is coordinated with an organized criminal group or planned enterprise.

modifiers.label: High Value Loss
modifiers.description: Applies when the offense involves a high-value loss threshold set by the active penal code.

modifiers.label: Prior Conviction
modifiers.description: Applies when the person has prior relevant convictions or a documented history of similar criminal conduct.

tiers.label: 1 Prior Conviction
tiers.description: Use when the person has one prior relevant conviction or similar documented criminal finding.

tiers.label: 2-3 Prior Convictions
tiers.description: Use when the person's prior convictions or similar documented findings show a pattern but have not reached the highest escalation tier.

tiers.label: 4+ Prior Convictions
tiers.description: Use when the person has an established repeated pattern of prior relevant convictions or similar documented findings.

modifiers.label: Minimal Participation
modifiers.description: Applies when the person's role in the offense was minor, limited, or substantially less culpable than other involved parties.

modifiers.label: Voluntary Surrender
modifiers.description: Applies when the person voluntarily surrenders, self-reports, or otherwise submits to lawful custody without requiring pursuit or force.

modifiers.label: First Offense
modifiers.description: Applies when the person has no relevant prior convictions or comparable documented criminal history.

modifiers.label: Citation Discretion
modifiers.description: Applies when officer discretion or policy makes a reduced citation appropriate.

modifiers.label: Gang Related Activity
modifiers.description: Applies when the offense is connected to or motivated by gang activity associated with the defendant.

charges.title: Assault
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Assault.
charges.migrationNotes: Assault with a Deadly Weapon was converted to weapon-used modifier eligibility; source PC-1004 is preserved on the modifier.

degrees.label: Simple Assault
degrees.description: A person who intentionally puts another in the reasonable belief of imminent physical harm or offensive contact is guilty under this code section.

legalPrinciples.overview: Simple assault focuses on whether the suspect intentionally created a reasonable fear of imminent physical harm or offensive contact.
principles: Physical contact is not required if the victim reasonably believed harm was imminent.
The threat must be more than vague or future-facing.
Officer reports should document words, gestures, distance, weapons, and victim reaction.

cases.title: State v. Example
cases.summary: Clarifies that immediate threatening conduct can satisfy assault even without completed battery.

resources.label: Assault Charging Guide
resources.note: Internal DOJ guidance for distinguishing assault, battery, and aggravated assault.

degrees.label: Assault
degrees.description: A person who intentionally puts another in the reasonable belief of imminent serious physical harm or offensive contact is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Aggravated Assault
degrees.description: A person who uses intentional and unlawful force or violence to cause physical harm to another person is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Battery
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Battery.
charges.migrationNotes: Battery on a Public Servant or Peace Officer was converted to public-servant-victim modifier eligibility; source PC-1029 is preserved on the modifier.

degrees.label: Battery
degrees.description: A person who unlawfully applies force directly or indirectly upon another person or their personal belongings, causing bodily injury or offensive contact is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Aggravated Battery
degrees.description: A person who intentionally and unlawfully applies force directly or indirectly upon another person or their personal belongings, causing bodily injury or offensive contact is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Manslaughter
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Manslaughter.

degrees.label: Involuntary Manslaughter
degrees.description: A person who unintentionally kills another, with or without a quarrel or heat of passion is guilty under this code section. A person who, through a criminal accident or negligence, causes someones death is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Vehicular Manslaughter
degrees.description: A person who, while operating a vehicle, through a criminal accident or negligence, causes someones death is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Murder
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Murder.
charges.migrationNotes: Murder of a Public Servant or Peace Officer was converted to public-servant-victim modifier eligibility; source PC-1014 is preserved on the modifier. Public-servant attempted/accessory source options remain attached to First Degree Murder.

degrees.label: Second Degree Murder
degrees.description: A person who unlawfully kills another person either by intentional malice or reckless disregard that occurs in the spur of the moment is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: First Degree Murder
degrees.description: A person who commits the intentional killing which is done in a way that is willful, deliberate and premeditated is guilty under this code section. Additionally, a person who kills another individual while engaging in a felony offense, that has been proved to be a premeditated act, is guilty under this code section.

liabilityOptions.label: Accessory to the Murder of a Public Servant or Peace Officer
liabilityOptions.sourceTitle: Accessory to the Murder of a Public Servant or Peace Officer

liabilityOptions.label: Attempted Murder of a Public Servant or Peace Officer
liabilityOptions.sourceTitle: Attempted Murder of a Public Servant or Peace Officer

charges.title: Unlawful Imprisonment
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Unlawful Imprisonment.
charges.migrationNotes: Unlawful Imprisonment of a Public Servant or Peace Officer was converted to public-servant-victim modifier eligibility; source PC-1023 is preserved on the modifier.

degrees.label: Unlawful Imprisonment
degrees.description: A person who intentionally restricts anothers freedom of movement without their consent is guilty under this code section

charges.title: Kidnapping and Hostage Taking
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Kidnapping and Hostage Taking.

degrees.label: Kidnapping
degrees.description: A person who abducts or confines another individual against their will by force, threat, or deception, is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Hostage Taking
degrees.description: A person who kidnaps someone in an attempt to gain the power to attain something, with threat of their life is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Criminal Threats
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Criminal Threats.

degrees.label: Criminal Threats
degrees.description: A person who communicates to another that they will physically harm or kill such other, placing such other in a reasonable state of fear for their own safety is guilty under this code section. Such communication can be not just verbal, but also in writing or transmitted through other media is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Reckless Endangerment
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Reckless Endangerment.

degrees.label: Reckless Endangerment
degrees.description: A person who consciously disregards the potential risks or dangers of their actions which create a substantial serious risk of injury to another person is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Desecration of a Human Corpse
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Desecration of a Human Corpse.

degrees.label: Desecration of a Human Corpse
degrees.description: Any act committed after the death of a human being including, but not limited to, dismemberment, disfigurement, mutilation, burning, or any act committed to cause the dead body to be devoured, scattered or dissipated

charges.title: Torture
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Torture.

degrees.label: Torture
degrees.description: A person who intentionally causes extreme pain and suffering to someone for reasons such as punishment, extracting a confession, interrogation, revenge, extortion, or any sadistic purpose, is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Theft by Property Value
charges.description: Theft offenses based on the value or protected class of personal property taken without consent.

degrees.label: Petty Theft
degrees.description: A person commits Petty Theft when they knowingly take, steal, carry away, or exercise control over another person's personal property valued at $2,000 or less without consent and with intent to deprive the owner of its use or value.

legalPrinciples.overview: Petty Theft covers lower-value property takings where consent and intent to deprive are the key facts.
principles: The property must belong to another person, business, or entity.
The value must be $2,000 or less based on available receipts, market value, or reasonable estimate.
Reports should document ownership, lack of consent, property value, recovery status, and suspect intent.

degrees.label: Grand Theft
degrees.description: A person commits Grand Theft when they knowingly take, steal, carry away, or exercise control over another person's personal property valued at more than $2,000 but less than $15,000, or a firearm of any value, without consent and with intent to deprive the owner of its use or value.

legalPrinciples.overview: Grand Theft applies to mid-value property takings and firearm theft regardless of firearm value.
principles: The value threshold must be supported by evidence such as receipts, market comparisons, or owner statements.
A stolen firearm qualifies even when its value is below the ordinary grand-theft threshold.
Reports should distinguish simple property taking from robbery, burglary, or vehicle theft when those elements are present.

degrees.label: Grand Larceny
degrees.description: A person commits Grand Larceny when they knowingly take, steal, carry away, or exercise control over another person's personal property valued at more than $15,000 without consent and with intent to deprive the owner of its use or value.

legalPrinciples.overview: Grand Larceny is the highest ordinary property-value theft tier.
principles: The property value must exceed $15,000.
Use the best available valuation evidence and document how value was calculated.
If the taking involved force, entry into a structure, or a vehicle, consider whether another theft-family offense better fits the conduct.

charges.title: Vehicle Theft
charges.description: Vehicle theft offenses involving unlawful taking or control of motor vehicles, aircraft, or protected government vehicles.
charges.migrationNotes: Includes vehicle-specific theft degrees; carjacking is included because the core act is vehicle theft from a person.

degrees.label: Grand Theft Auto A
degrees.description: A person commits Grand Theft Auto A when they knowingly take, drive, operate, move, or exercise control over another person's motor vehicle without consent and with intent to temporarily or permanently deprive the owner of possession or use.

legalPrinciples.overview: Grand Theft Auto A covers unlawful taking or control of a motor vehicle without aggravating circumstances.
principles: Vehicle value is not required for this degree.
The suspect must lack consent from the owner or lawful possessor.
Reports should document vehicle ownership, how control was taken, location recovered, damage, and any statements showing intent.

degrees.label: Grand Theft Auto B
degrees.description: A person commits Grand Theft Auto B when they commit motor vehicle theft while armed, while committing another felony, or while using the stolen vehicle to facilitate another felony offense.

legalPrinciples.overview: Grand Theft Auto B is the aggravated motor-vehicle theft tier.
principles: The vehicle theft elements must be satisfied first.
The aggravating fact must be tied to the theft event, such as being armed or committing another felony.
Reports should document the separate felony or weapon facts instead of relying only on the vehicle theft.

degrees.label: Carjacking
degrees.description: A person commits Carjacking when they unlawfully take or attempt to take a motor vehicle from another person or the person's immediate presence while the vehicle is occupied, controlled, or being operated.

legalPrinciples.overview: Carjacking focuses on vehicle theft from a person rather than unattended vehicle theft.
principles: The vehicle must be taken from a person, their immediate presence, or while being operated.
Force, intimidation, or threats should be documented when present, even if not required by the short title.
Reports should identify the occupant, driver, or person in immediate control of the vehicle.

degrees.label: Theft of an Aircraft
degrees.description: A person commits Theft of an Aircraft when they knowingly take, operate, move, or exercise control over an aircraft without consent of the owner or lawful operator and with intent to deprive the owner or operator of possession or use.

legalPrinciples.overview: Theft of an Aircraft is a protected vehicle-theft degree for aircraft.
principles: Aircraft status should be documented by registration, ownership, or operational control.
Consent from the owner or authorized operator is the central issue.
Reports should document flight, attempted flight, movement, damage, recovery, and safety risks created by the theft.

degrees.label: Theft of a Law Enforcement Vehicle
degrees.sourceTitle: Theft of a Law Enforcement Vehicle
degrees.description: A person commits Theft of a Law Enforcement Vehicle when they knowingly take, operate, move, or exercise control over a motor vehicle owned, assigned, or operated by a law enforcement agency without authorization.

legalPrinciples.overview: Theft of a Law Enforcement Vehicle is a protected vehicle-theft degree based on agency ownership or use.
principles: The vehicle must be tied to a law enforcement agency by ownership, assignment, or official use.
The suspect must lack authorization to possess or operate it.
Reports should document agency ownership, equipment present, recovery status, and any public-safety risk.

charges.title: Burglary
charges.description: Unlawful entry into a structure with intent to commit a criminal offense or remain without permission.

degrees.label: Burglary
degrees.description: A person commits Burglary when they enter or remain in a structure, room, secured area, or enclosed property without permission and with intent to commit a criminal offense inside.

legalPrinciples.overview: Burglary focuses on unlawful entry or remaining with criminal intent.
principles: Unlawful entry alone is not enough; document the intended or completed offense.
Permission may be limited by time, place, purpose, or revocation.
Reports should document entry method, owner or agent statements, security footage, tools, stolen property, and suspect intent.

charges.title: Robbery
charges.description: Theft from a person or immediate presence by force, intimidation, fear, or threat.
charges.migrationNotes: Armed Robbery was converted to weapon-used modifier eligibility; source PC-2010 is preserved on the modifier. Armed robbery attempted/accessory source options remain attached to Robbery.

degrees.label: Robbery
degrees.description: A person commits Robbery when they unlawfully take or attempt to take personal property from another person, business, or the person's immediate presence against their will by force, intimidation, fear, or threat.

legalPrinciples.overview: Robbery is theft elevated by force, intimidation, fear, or taking from immediate presence.
principles: The property must be taken from a person, business, or immediate presence against the victim's will.
Force or intimidation must be connected to the taking, escape, or retention of property.
If a weapon or dangerous instrument was used, apply the weapon-used modifier rather than creating a separate armed degree.

charges.title: Leaving Without Paying
charges.description: Leaving a billed premises without paying for goods or services received.

degrees.label: Leaving Without Paying
degrees.description: A person commits Leaving Without Paying when they receive goods, services, food, lodging, transport, or another billed benefit and knowingly leave or attempt to leave without paying the amount due.

legalPrinciples.overview: Leaving Without Paying covers low-level theft of billed goods or services.
principles: The person must have received or accepted a billed good or service.
The nonpayment must be knowing rather than a good-faith billing dispute.
Reports should document the bill, amount due, opportunity to pay, suspect departure, and any statements showing intent.

charges.title: Possession of Nonlegal Currency
charges.description: Possession, use, or attempted use of fraudulent currency represented as lawful money.

degrees.label: Possession of Nonlegal Currency
degrees.description: A person commits Possession of Nonlegal Currency when they knowingly possess, pass, attempt to pass, create, or use fraudulent currency while representing it as lawful tender.

legalPrinciples.overview: This degree covers counterfeit or fraudulent currency handled as if it were legal money.
principles: The item must be represented or intended to be used as lawful currency.
Mere accidental receipt may not be enough without knowledge or attempted use.
Reports should document the currency, transaction context, suspect statements, and any additional counterfeit items or tools.

charges.title: Possession of Government-Issued Items
charges.description: Unauthorized possession of government-issued property, equipment, vehicles, weapons, or identification-like items.

degrees.label: Possession of Government-Issued Items
degrees.description: A person commits Possession of Government-Issued Items when they knowingly possess a government-issued firearm, vehicle, equipment item, credential, or other official property without lawful authority or permission.

legalPrinciples.overview: This degree covers unauthorized possession of official government property.
principles: The item must be government-issued, government-owned, or restricted to official use.
The suspect must lack permission, assignment, or lawful authority to possess it.
Reports should document the item type, issuing agency, how possession was discovered, and whether the item was recovered.

charges.title: Items Used in the Commission of a Crime
charges.description: Possession or sale of tools, equipment, or items intended for use in committing criminal offenses.
charges.migrationNotes: Grouped by title family; PC-2018 description appears to duplicate nonlegal currency text and should be reviewed later.

degrees.label: Possession of Items Used in the Commission of a Crime
degrees.sourceTitle: Possession of Items Used in the Commission of a Crime
degrees.description: A person commits Possession of Items Used in the Commission of a Crime when they knowingly possess tools, equipment, weapons, devices, or materials intended for use in committing, attempting, or facilitating a criminal offense.

legalPrinciples.overview: This degree covers possession of tools or equipment tied to criminal conduct.
principles: Possession should be connected to an intended, attempted, or completed offense.
Ordinary items require contextual evidence showing criminal use or intent.
Reports should document the item, location, related offense, suspect statements, and why lawful use is unlikely.

degrees.label: Sale of Items Used in the Commission of a Crime
degrees.sourceTitle: Sale of Items Used in the Commission of a Crime
degrees.description: A person commits Sale of Items Used in the Commission of a Crime when they knowingly sell, offer, transfer, or distribute tools, equipment, weapons, devices, or materials with knowledge or reason to know they will be used to commit or facilitate a criminal offense.

legalPrinciples.overview: This degree covers trafficking or distribution of crime-facilitating items.
principles: The item must be connected to criminal use, not merely capable of lawful use.
Knowledge may be shown by statements, packaging, context, repeated transactions, or the nature of the item.
Reports should document the sale or transfer, buyer/seller roles, communications, item function, and related offense.

charges.title: Criminal Possession of Stolen Property
charges.description: Possession or control of property known to be stolen.

degrees.label: Criminal Possession of Stolen Property
degrees.description: A person commits Criminal Possession of Stolen Property when they knowingly possess, receive, conceal, transport, sell, or control property while knowing or reasonably believing the property was stolen.

legalPrinciples.overview: This degree focuses on knowing possession or control of stolen property after the theft.
principles: The state must show the property was stolen and the suspect knew or should reasonably have known it.
Possession may be physical, constructive, shared, or through control over storage or transport.
Reports should document theft reports, serial numbers, owner identification, recovery location, and suspect explanation.

charges.title: Customs and Smuggling Offenses
charges.description: Import, export, transport, concealment, or misrepresentation of goods in violation of customs or licensing requirements.
charges.migrationNotes: Accessory to smuggling becomes a liability option; customs fraud and unlicensed import/export remain degrees in the same import/export family.

degrees.label: Smuggling of Contraband
degrees.description: A person commits Smuggling of Contraband when they knowingly transport, import, export, conceal, or move contraband goods across a jurisdictional boundary or customs-controlled area without required documentation, licensing, declaration, or authorization.

legalPrinciples.overview: This degree covers general contraband smuggling across customs or jurisdictional controls.
principles: The goods must be contraband or otherwise subject to declaration, licensing, or customs control.
The conduct must involve transport, import, export, concealment, or movement through a controlled boundary.
Reports should document the goods, route, concealment method, missing paperwork, declarations made, and all involved parties.

degrees.label: Smuggling of Precious Gemstones
degrees.description: A person commits Smuggling of Precious Gemstones when they knowingly transport, import, export, conceal, or move precious gemstones across a jurisdictional boundary or customs-controlled area without required documentation, licensing, declaration, or authorization.

legalPrinciples.overview: This degree is the gemstone-specific smuggling tier.
principles: Covered gemstones include diamonds, emeralds, rubies, sapphires, and other valuable stones.
Documentation should establish the type, quantity, value, and lack of lawful declaration.
Reports should document packaging, concealment, valuation, route, ownership claims, and associated business records.

degrees.label: Smuggling of Precious Metals
degrees.description: A person commits Smuggling of Precious Metals when they knowingly transport, import, export, conceal, or move precious metals across a jurisdictional boundary or customs-controlled area without required documentation, licensing, declaration, or authorization.

legalPrinciples.overview: This degree is the precious-metal-specific smuggling tier.
principles: Covered metals include gold, silver, platinum, and comparable valuable metals.
Documentation should establish the type, quantity, value, and lack of lawful declaration.
Reports should document weight, purity if known, concealment method, route, ownership claims, and associated business records.

degrees.label: Customs Fraud
degrees.description: A person commits Customs Fraud when they knowingly provide false information, false documentation, false valuation, false quantity, or other material misrepresentation to customs or government authorities to evade duties, taxes, licensing, declaration, inspection, or regulatory requirements.

legalPrinciples.overview: Customs Fraud focuses on false statements or documents used to evade customs or regulatory controls.
principles: The false statement or document must be material to customs, tax, licensing, or inspection decisions.
The suspect must know or intend that the information is false or misleading.
Reports should preserve forms, invoices, manifests, declarations, communications, valuation evidence, and officer observations.

degrees.label: Operating an Unlicensed Import/Export Business
degrees.description: A person commits Operating an Unlicensed Import/Export Business when they conduct commercial import or export activity without required licensing, permits, registration, or regulatory authorization, including repeated or high-value shipments, dedicated business infrastructure, or transport personnel knowingly participating in the operation.

legalPrinciples.overview: This degree covers commercial-scale import/export activity conducted outside required licensing or regulatory oversight.
principles: Commercial scale may be shown by high-value goods, repeated transactions, large shipment weight, business infrastructure, or profit-driven operations.
Transportation personnel are included when they knowingly participate in the unlicensed operation.
Reports should document licenses checked, shipment values, transaction frequency, business records, transport roles, and regulatory notices.

charges.title: Impersonation
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Impersonation.

degrees.label: Impersonating
degrees.description: A person who attempts to assume the identity of someone else is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Impersonating a Peace Officer or Public Servant
degrees.sourceTitle: Impersonating a Peace Officer or Public Servant
degrees.description: A person who attempts to assume the identity, or state that they are a peace officer or public servant, when they are not, are guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Impersonating a Judge
degrees.description: A person who attempts to assume the identity, or state that they are a judge, when they are not, are guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Possession of Stolen Government Identification
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Possession of Stolen Government Identification.

degrees.label: Possession of Stolen Government Identification
degrees.description: A person who is in possession of a piece of government identification that does not belong to them, who has not made any attempt to dispose of the item, is guilty under this section.

charges.title: Extortion
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Extortion.

degrees.label: Extortion
degrees.description: A person who intimidates or influences another to provide or hand over properties or services is guilty under this code section. A person who utilizes or threatens their power or authority with demonstrated malice aforethought in order to compel action by another is guilty under this code section

charges.title: Fraud and Forgery
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Fraud and Forgery.

degrees.label: Fraud
degrees.description: A person who knowingly alters, creates, or uses a written document with the intent to defraud or deceive another is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Forgery
degrees.description: A person who knowingly signs a document or agreement, electronic or otherwise, without the consent or authority of whom they are signing for is guilty under this code section. A person who creates fake government documents is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Money Laundering
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Money Laundering.

degrees.label: Money Laundering
degrees.description: A person who possesses, hides, transfers, receives, or maintains the storage of funds earned through comprehensive criminal activities is guilty under this code. A person who maintains an establishment with a purpose to launder funds collected through comprehensive criminal activities is guilty under this code.

charges.title: Trespassing
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Trespassing.

degrees.label: Trespassing
degrees.description: A person who remains on a property after being told to leave by the property owner, an agent of the property owner, or a peace officer, or returns to a property after having been previously trespassed from the property is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Felony Trespassing
degrees.description: A person who, without proper authorization, enters any government-owned or managed facility that is secured with the intent of keeping ordinary citizens outside is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Arson
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Arson.

degrees.label: Arson
degrees.description: A person who intentionally and maliciously sets fire to or burns any structure, forest land, or property without prior authorization is guilty under this code section. A person who intentionally aids, counsels, or helps facilitate the burning of any structure, forest land, or property without proper authorization is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Vandalism
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Vandalism.

degrees.label: Vandalism
degrees.description: A person that defaces, damages, or destroys property which belongs to another is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Vandalism of Government Property
degrees.description: A person that defaces, damages, or destroys property which belongs to a government agency is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Littering
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Littering.

degrees.label: Littering
degrees.description: As used in this section, "litter" means garbage, trash, waste, ashes, cans, bottles, wire, paper, cartons, vessel parts, vehicle parts, furniture, glass, or anything else of an unsightly or unsanitary nature. No person shall place any waste, refuse, litter or foreign substance in any area or receptacle except those provided for that purpose.

charges.title: Bribery of a Government Official
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Bribery of a Government Official.

degrees.label: Bribery of a Government Official
degrees.description: A person who offers or gives a monetary gift, gratuity, valuable goods, or other reward to a public official, a government employee, or peace officer in an attempt to influence their duties or actions is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Anti-Mask Law
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Anti-Mask Law.

degrees.label: Anti-Mask Law
degrees.description: A person who wears a mask or face covering while committing a crime is guilty under this code section. A person who wears a mask in a government facility, after being asked to remove it, is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Possession of Contraband in a Government Facility
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Possession of Contraband in a Government Facility.

degrees.label: Possession of Contraband in a Government Facility
degrees.sourceTitle: Possession of Contraband in a Government Facility
degrees.description: A person who possesses a controlled substance, illegal firearm, or any other illegal item while on the premesis of a government facility is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Escape and Jailbreak
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Escape and Jailbreak.

degrees.label: Escaping
degrees.description: Any person arrested, detained, booked, charged, or convicted of any crime who thereafter escapes from a county or city jail, prison, community service, or custody of a Correctional or Parole Officer, Peace Officer, Police Officer, or Federal Agent is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Jailbreak
degrees.description: A person who breaks out a prisoner from a correctional facility without authorization is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Perjury
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Perjury.

degrees.label: Perjury
degrees.description: A person who willfully gives false information while testifying in court, during a deposition, or on a signed document presented to a court is guilty under this section.

charges.title: Court Order and Appearance Violations
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Court Order and Appearance Violations.
charges.migrationNotes: Grouped as court compliance conduct while preserving each source as its own degree.

degrees.label: Violation of a Restraining Order
degrees.description: A person who knowingly and intentionally violates the parameters of a restraining order against them is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Violating a Court Order
degrees.description: A person who fails to abide by a court order ruled by a judge of San Andreas is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Failure to Appear
degrees.description: A person who fails to appear to a lawfully binding court summons or order for appearance is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Contempt of Court
degrees.description: A person who is disrespectful of the court process, such as being excessively loud or belligerent, refusing to be sworn in as a witness, refusing to comply with a judges request, is guilty under this code section. Repeated offenses can result in multiplication of the maximum fine and sentence.

charges.title: Embezzlement
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Embezzlement.

degrees.label: Embezzlement
degrees.description: A person who steals or misappropriates funds in their trust or belonging to their employer is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Unlawful Practice
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Unlawful Practice.

degrees.label: Unlawful Practice
degrees.description: A person who practices medical procedures that they are not licenced or lawfully allowed to practice is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Misuse of Emergency Systems
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Misuse of Emergency Systems.

degrees.label: Misuse of Emergency Systems
degrees.description: A person who misuses an emergency system, such as 911 or panic buttons, to waste police time or resources, is guilty under this code section

charges.title: Conspiracy
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Conspiracy.

degrees.label: Conspiracy
degrees.description: A person who conspires to commit a crime, either alone or with a group, is guilty under this section. A person charged with this can be charged up to half of the fine and sentence of the conspired crime.

charges.title: Resisting Arrest
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Resisting Arrest.

degrees.label: Resisting Arrest
degrees.description: A person who avoids apprehension from an officer by non-vehicular means or resists apprehension by any physical means is guilty under this code section is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Peace Officer Compliance
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Peace Officer Compliance.

degrees.label: Disobeying a Peace Officer
degrees.description: A person who fails to comply with a lawful order given from an on duty peace officer or public servant is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Failure to Provide Identification
degrees.description: A person who fails to identify when lawfully ordered to by a Law Enforcement Officer is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Public Disorder
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Public Disorder.
charges.migrationNotes: Includes escalating public-order conduct from disorder through riot incitement/unlawful assembly.

degrees.label: Disorderly Conduct
degrees.description: A person who commits such acts that are of a nature to corrupt the public morals, or outrage the sense of public decency, or affect the peace and quiet of persons who may witness them, or engages in brawling or fighting, or engages in such conduct as to constitute a breach of the peace is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Disturbing the Peace
degrees.description: A person who violates a reasonable expectation of peace in a public area is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Inciting a Riot
degrees.description: A person who with the intent to cause a riot does an act or engages in conduct that urges a riot, or urges others to commit acts of force or violence, or the burning or destroying of property, and at a time and place and under circumstances that produce a clear and present and immediate danger of acts of force or violence or the burning or destroying of property is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Unlawful Assembly
degrees.description: Whenever two or more persons, assembled and acting together, make any attempt or advance toward the commission of an act which would be a riot if actually committed. Whenever two or more persons assemble together to do an unlawful act, or do a lawful act in a violent, boisterous, or tumultuous manner is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: False Reporting
charges.description: Grouped charge family for False Reporting.

degrees.label: False Reporting
degrees.description: A person who reports to any peace officer that a felony or misdemeanor has been committed knowing the report to be false is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Harassment and Stalking
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Harassment and Stalking.

degrees.label: Harassment
degrees.description: A person who makes communication, whether in person or by means of internet, phone, or other devices (may also apply to circumventing a block on a phone number) with the repeated intent to cause annoyance.

degrees.label: Stalking
degrees.description: A person who intentionally and maliciously follows or harasses another person who has made it known that they do not consent to such following or harassment is guilty under this code section. A person whose actions cause another person to reasonably fear for their safety, or the safety of any person is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Obstruction of Justice
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Obstruction of Justice.

degrees.label: Misdemeanor Obstruction of Justice
degrees.description: A person who shows a clear and motivated attempt to prevent a peace officer from conducting their duties or completing an investigation is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Felony Obstruction of Justice
degrees.description: A person who shows a clear and motivated attempt to prevent an official government proceeding or government officer from completing their assigned duties is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Loitering on Government Properties
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Loitering on Government Properties.

degrees.label: Loitering on Government Properties
degrees.description: Criminal loitering refers to anyone who is lingering or hanging around in a public or private area, with the intent to commit criminal activity, or who is assisting and/or aiding another with a crime

charges.title: Vehicle Tampering
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Vehicle Tampering.

degrees.label: Vehicle Tampering
degrees.description: A person who intentionally tampers or damages a vehicle without the consent of the owner is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Tampering
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Tampering.

degrees.label: Evidence Tampering
degrees.description: A person who willfully and intentionally destroys or attempts to destroy, creates or attempts to create false evidence, conceal, or alter any evidence that can later potentially be used in a Criminal investigation or court proceeding is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Witness Tampering
degrees.description: This pertains to a person who knowingly and maliciously prevents or encourages any witness or victim from attending or giving testimony at any trial, proceeding, or inquiry authorized by law with the use of bribery, fear, or other tactics.

charges.title: Vigilantism
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Vigilantism.

degrees.label: Vigilantism
degrees.description: A person who unlawfully attempts to enforce law, or act as law enforcement, is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Government Corruption
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Government Corruption.

degrees.label: Government Corruption
degrees.description: The deliberate abuse of authority by any public official, government employee, or elected representative for personal, financial, political, or organizational gain. This includes, but is not limited to: accepting bribes, engaging in quid pro quo arrangements, falsifying official records, misappropriating public resources, interfering with law enforcement investigations, or using official status to shield oneself or others from lawful accountability.

charges.title: Aiding and Harboring
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Aiding and Harboring.

degrees.label: Aiding and Abetting
degrees.description: A person who assists in the committing of a crime, or assists in the fleeing of a wanted person is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Harboring a Fugitive
degrees.description: A person who knowingly and intentionally hides, harbours or prevents law enforcement from finding a wanted felon is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Marijuana Offenses
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Marijuana Offenses.

degrees.label: Illegal Cultivation of Marijuana
degrees.description: Any individual who is found to be cultivating marijuana in an amount greater than 6 marijuana plants or is cultivating marijuana on public property is in violation of this code. Cultivation of marijuana plants for personal use is restricted to private property, and no greater than 6 plants per property/owner. Plants can be confiscated and/or destroyed by police upon receipt of warrant (for private property) for seizure.

degrees.label: Illegal Cultivation of Marijuana
degrees.description: Any individual who is found to be cultivating marijuana in an amount greater than 6 marijuana plants under the following conditions is classified as a felony: If the individual has two or more prior convictions for cultivating more than six marijuana plants, if the individual has a prior conviction for a serious violent felony, and or the cultivation activity involves violation of environmental laws. Cultivation of marijuana plants for personal use is restricted to private property, and no greater than 6 plants per property/owner. Plants can be confiscated and/or destroyed by police upon receipt of a warrant (private property) for seizure.

degrees.label: Possession of Marijuana
degrees.description: A person who is in possession of illegal marijuana that weighs more than 10 kg, but less than 100 kg is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Possession of Marijuana with Intent to Distribute
degrees.sourceTitle: Possession of Marijuana with Intent to Distribute
degrees.description: A person found in possession of over 100 kg of illegal marijuana, with more than 30 kg of marijuana individually packaged for sale (e.g., in joints, baggies, or bricks), and/or also possesses items commonly used in drug distribution (such as scales, empty baggies, etc.), weighing more than 50 kg, is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Cocaine Possession and Distribution
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Cocaine Possession and Distribution.

degrees.label: Misdemeanor Possession of Cocaine
degrees.description: A person who is in possession of cocaine, either in powder or crack formulations, under 10 kg is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Felony Possession of Cocaine
degrees.description: A person who is in possession of cocaine, either in powder or crack formulations, greater than 10kg but less than 100kg is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Possession of Cocaine with Intent to Distribute
degrees.sourceTitle: Possession of Cocaine with Intent to Distribute
degrees.description: A person who is in possession of cocaine, either in powder or crack formulations, that exceeds 100 kg in weight, or is packaged individually for sale, and possesses items used in the distribution of drugs (ie. scale, empty baggies, etc.) and weighs more than 50 kg, is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Amphetamine Possession and Distribution
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Amphetamine Possession and Distribution.

degrees.label: Misdemeanor Possession of Amphetamines
degrees.description: A person who is in possession of amphetamines, including but not limited to methamphetamine and adderall, under 10 kg is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Felony Possession of Amphetamines
degrees.description: A person who is in possession of amphetamines, including but not limited to methamphetamine and adderall, greater than 10kg but less than 100kg is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Possession of Amphetamines with Intent to Distribute
degrees.sourceTitle: Possession of Amphetamines with Intent to Distribute
degrees.description: A person who is in possession of amphetamines, including but not limited to methamphetamine and adderall, that exceeds 100 kg in weight, or is packaged individually for sale, and possesses items used in the distribution of drugs (ie. scale, empty baggies, etc.) and weighs more than 50 kg, is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Opioid Possession and Distribution
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Opioid Possession and Distribution.

degrees.label: Misdemeanor Possession of Opioids
degrees.description: A person who is in possession of opioids, including but not limited to morphine, heroin, hydrocodone, oxycodone, under 10 kg is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Felony Possession of Opioids
degrees.description: A person who is in possession of opioids, including but not limited to morphine, heroin, hydrocodone, oxycodone, greater than 10kg but less than 100kg is guilty under this code section

degrees.label: Possession of Opioids with Intent to Distribute
degrees.sourceTitle: Possession of Opioids with Intent to Distribute
degrees.description: A person who is in possession of amphetamines, including but not limited to opioids, including but not limited to morphine, heroin, hydrocodone, oxycodone, or is packaged individually for sale, and possesses items used in the distribution of drugs (ie. scale, empty baggies, etc.) and weighs more than 50 kg, is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Possession of Drug Paraphernalia
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Possession of Drug Paraphernalia.

degrees.label: Possession of Drug Paraphernalia
degrees.description: A person who is in possession of any equipment, product or material of any kind which is primarily intended or designed for use in injecting, ingesting, inhaling, or otherwise introducing into the human body a controlled substance.

charges.title: Possession of Drug Manufacturing Materials
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Possession of Drug Manufacturing Materials.

degrees.label: Possession of Drug Manufacturing Materials
degrees.description: A person who is in possession of any equipment, product or material of any kind which could be used in manufacturing, compounding, converting, concealing, producing, processing, preparing a controlled substance.

charges.title: Controlled Substance Sale and Trafficking
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Controlled Substance Sale and Trafficking.

degrees.label: Sale of a controlled substance
degrees.description: A person who sells, offers to sell, transports with the intent to sell, or gives away a controlled substance to another person, regardless of whether or not they possess that controlled substance is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Drug Trafficking
degrees.description: A person who unlawfully transports a large quantity of a controlled substance, defined as greater than 250 kg, across, into, or out of the state is guilty under this section.

charges.title: Driving Under the Influence of Narcotics
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Driving Under the Influence of Narcotics.

degrees.label: Driving Under the Influence of Narcotics
degrees.sourceTitle: Driving Under the Influence of Narcotics
degrees.description: A person who operates a motor vehicle on a public roadway while under the influence of narcotics, or other medicine that inhibits your ability to drive safely is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Public Intoxication
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Public Intoxication.

degrees.label: Public Intoxication
degrees.description: A person who is under the influence of alcohol in a public place, and disturbing the natural expectation of peace, is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Public Indecency
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Public Indecency.

degrees.label: Public Indecency
degrees.description: A person who fails to appropriately dress themselves in a public place, or displays themselves to unconsenting parties in public areas, is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Moonshine Offenses
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Moonshine Offenses.

degrees.label: Unlawful Production of Moonshine
degrees.description: The act of manufacturing, distilling, or fermenting alcoholic beverages, such as moonshine, without proper licensing or permits as required by law.

degrees.label: Possession or Distribution of Illegal Moonshine
degrees.sourceTitle: Possession or Distribution of Illegal Moonshine
degrees.description: The act of possessing, transporting, or distributing unlicensed alcoholic beverages, such as moonshine, with the intent to sell or consume.

charges.title: Criminal Possession of Weapons
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Criminal Possession of Weapons.

degrees.label: Criminal Possession of Weapon Class A
degrees.sourceTitle: Criminal Possession of Weapon Class A
degrees.description: A person who illegally possesses a weapon of Class A is guilty under this code section. Illegal possession is defined as someone who is not licensed to or legally able to carry a weapon based on their criminal history. Class A weapons are defined as melee or blunt instruments that are designed to inflict damage, defined to include switchblades, knife, or brass knuckles. The charge of possessing a Class A weapon is a secondary offense.

degrees.label: Criminal Possession of Weapon Class B
degrees.sourceTitle: Criminal Possession of Weapon Class B
degrees.description: A person who illegally possesses a weapon of Class B is guilty under this code section. Illegal possession is defined as someone who is not licensed to or legally able to carry a weapon based on their criminal history. Class B weapons are defined as semi-automatic handguns or rifles.

degrees.label: Criminal Possession of Weapon Class C
degrees.sourceTitle: Criminal Possession of Weapon Class C
degrees.description: A person who illegally possesses a weapon of Class C is guilty under this code section. Illegal possession is defined as someone who is not licensed to or legally able to carry a weapon based on their criminal history. Class C weapons are defined as automatic handguns or rifles.

degrees.label: Criminal Possession of Weapon Class D
degrees.sourceTitle: Criminal Possession of Weapon Class D
degrees.description: A person who illegally possesses a weapon of Class D is guilty under this code section. Illegal possession is defined as someone who is not licensed to or legally able to carry a weapon based on their criminal history. Class D weapons are defined as heavy artillery and explosives, including rocket launchers, C4, grenades, home-made explosives.

charges.title: Criminal Sale of Weapons
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Criminal Sale of Weapons.

degrees.label: Criminal Sale of Weapon Class A
degrees.sourceTitle: Criminal Sale of Weapon Class A
degrees.description: A person who illegally sells or distributes a weapon of Class A is guilty under this code section. Class A weapons are defined as melee or blunt instruments that are designed to inflict damage, defined to include switchblades, knife, or brass knuckles.

degrees.label: Criminal Sale of Weapon Class B
degrees.sourceTitle: Criminal Sale of Weapon Class B
degrees.description: A person who illegally sells or distributes a weapon of Class B is guilty under this code section. Illegal possession is defined as someone who is not licensed to or legally able to carry a weapon based on their criminal history. Class B weapons are defined as semi-automatic handguns or rifles.

degrees.label: Criminal Sale of Weapon Class C
degrees.sourceTitle: Criminal Sale of Weapon Class C
degrees.description: A person who illegally sells or distributes a weapon of Class C is guilty under this code section. Illegal possession is defined as someone who is not licensed to or legally able to carry a weapon based on their criminal history. Class C weapons are defined as automatic handguns or rifles.

degrees.label: Criminal Sale of Weapon Class D
degrees.sourceTitle: Criminal Sale of Weapon Class D
degrees.description: A person who illegally sells or distributes a weapon of Class D is guilty under this code section. Illegal possession is defined as someone who is not licensed to or legally able to carry a weapon based on their criminal history. Class D weapons are defined as heavy artillery and explosives, including rocket launchers, C4, grenades, home-made explosives.

charges.title: Criminal Use of Weapons and Explosives
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Criminal Use of Weapons and Explosives.

degrees.label: Criminal Use of Weapon
degrees.description: A person who uses a weapon or firearm in the process of committing a crime is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Criminal Use of Explosives
degrees.description: A person who uses explosives or incediaries in the act of committing a crime is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Illegal Firearm Configuration
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Illegal Firearm Configuration.

degrees.label: Possession of Illegal Firearm Modifications
degrees.description: A person who is in possession of a firearm with modifications that are illegal is guilty under this code section. Examples include Full Auto Switch, extended magazines, suppressors, and serial number removal.

degrees.label: Possession of Firearms Without Serial Numbers
degrees.sourceTitle: Possession of Firearms Without Serial Numbers
degrees.description: A person who possesses a firearm that does not have a registered serial number with the State of San Andreas, regardless of the individual's gun license status, is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Weapon Trafficking
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Weapon Trafficking.

degrees.label: Weapon Trafficking
degrees.description: A person who is responsible for the mass transportation (excess of 8) illegal firearms and weapons within or across the state of San Andreas with the intention of sale or distribution is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Illegal Manufacturing of Firearms
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Illegal Manufacturing of Firearms.

degrees.label: Illegal Manufacturing of Firearms
degrees.description: Any individual who manufactures a firearm must be eligible to own a firearm in the State. Any firearm manufactured in the State of San Andreas must be a legal weapon and meet all safety standards including containing an unique serial number that is registered with the State. San Andreas law prohibits individuals from manufacturing or assembling certain classes of firearms, including assault weapons and machine guns. The sale of self-made firearms is illegal. An individual found in violation is guilty of this code section..

charges.title: Brandishing Weapons
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Brandishing Weapons.

degrees.label: Brandishing a Weapon
degrees.description: A person who removes a weapon other than a firearm from concealment or holstering in a public place, either recklessly or threateningly, without obvious threat of harm or other lawful reason to use a weapon, is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Brandishing a Firearm
degrees.description: A person who removes a firearm from concealment or holstering in a public place, either recklessly or threateningly, without obvious threat of harm or other lawful reason to use a firearm is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Insurrection
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Insurrection.

degrees.label: Insurrection
degrees.description: A person who incites, sets on foot, assists, or engages in any rebellion or insurrection against the authority of the United States is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Flying into Restricted Airspace
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Flying into Restricted Airspace.

degrees.label: Flying into Restricted Airspace
degrees.description: A person who, while operating an aircraft, flies over restricted airspace, or flies into controlled airspace without prior authorization from air control, is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Jaywalking
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Jaywalking.

degrees.label: Jaywalking
degrees.description: A person who crosses a road not at a valid crossing, within 100 meters of a valid crossing, is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Improper Firearm Storage and Handling
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Improper Firearm Storage and Handling.

degrees.label: Improper Storage of Firearm
degrees.description: A person who stores, or is in possession of, a firearm in an unsecured container, such as a vehicle glovebox or unlocked vehicle trunk, is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Improper Handling of Firearm
degrees.description: A licensed person who fails to report a lost or stolen firearm; lends a firearm to another licensed person outside the presence of the registered owner; or provides firearm access to an unlicensed person is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Criminal Possession of Ammunition
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Criminal Possession of Ammunition.

degrees.label: Criminal Possession of Ammunition
degrees.description: A person who possesses ammunition without a firearms license, or who is otherwise legally prohibited from possessing ammunition due to their criminal history, is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Driving While Intoxicated
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Driving While Intoxicated.

degrees.label: Driving While Intoxicated
degrees.description: A person who operates a motor vehicle while under the influence of alcohol with a BAC over 0.08 is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Evading
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Evading.

degrees.label: Evading
degrees.description: A person who, while operating a vehicle on land, sea, or in air, or while operating a bicycle, willfully flees or otherwise attempts to elude or avoid a pursuing peace officer who communicates visually or audibly their request to pull over or stop is guilty under this code.

degrees.label: Reckless Evading
degrees.description: A person who, while operating a vehicle on land, sea, or in air, or while operating a bicycle, willfully flees or otherwise attempts to elude or avoid a pursuing peace officer in a reckless or dangerous manner, defined as failure to maintain correct lanes of travel, excessive variability in rates of speed (ie. brake checking), or shows an imminent threat to the wellbeing of the general public, is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Emergency and Traffic Control Compliance
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Emergency and Traffic Control Compliance.

degrees.label: Failure to Yield to Emergency Vehicle
degrees.sourceTitle: Failure to Yield to Emergency Vehicle
degrees.description: A person who, while operating a motor vehicle, fails to yeild when signalled to by emergency services lights and sirens is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Failure to Obey Traffic Control Device
degrees.sourceTitle: Failure to Obey Traffic Control Device
degrees.description: A person who fails to obey a traffic control device, such as a stop sign, traffic lights, or yield sign is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Vehicle Roadworthiness and Equipment
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Vehicle Roadworthiness and Equipment.

degrees.label: Unroadworthy Vehicle
degrees.description: A person who operates a motor vehicle on a public roadway that is not permitted for legal road use is guilty under this code section. Vehicles such as dirtbikes, tracked vehicles, racecars, golf carts, fall under this section. Additionally, vehicles that are not equipped with indicators, brake lights, or have excessive damage are also guilty under this section.

degrees.label: Driving without Headlights or Signals
degrees.description: A person who operates a motor vehicle in low light conditions or inclement weather conditions without their headlights switched on is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Illegal Vehicle Modifications
degrees.description: Owners and drivers of vehicles on public streets with upgrades that are not legal for street use, are guilty under this code section. Upgrades that are not legal for street use, but are legal for off-road or track use include: Engines that are Tier 4 or 5, Transmission 3 or 4, NOS, and bulletproof tires.

degrees.label: Commercial Vehicle Violation
degrees.description: An owner or driver of a commercial vehicle who fails a San Andreas Department of Transportation inspection is in violation of this code section.

charges.title: Unsafe Vehicle Operation
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Unsafe Vehicle Operation.

degrees.label: Negligent Driving
degrees.description: A person who, while operating a motor vehicle, displays behavior that endangers the safety of other drivers or pedestrians, due to negligence, is guilty under this code section. Negligent driving includes, but is not limited to, using a cellular device while driving on a public road or highway; and failing to move over or slow down for stationary emergency and service vehicles with warning lights flashing.

degrees.label: Reckless Driving
degrees.description: A person who, while operating a motor vehicle, displays behavior that endangers the safety of other drivers or pedestrians, due to recklessness, defined as failure to maintain correct lanes of travel, excessive variability in rates of speed (ie. brake checking), or shows an imminent threat to the wellbeing of the general public, is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Motor Vehicle Contest
degrees.description: A person who attempts to or engages in racing or vehicular contest on public roadways is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Public Disturbance by Motor Vehicle
degrees.description: Individuals who use a motor vehicle in a way that causes public disturbance are guilty under this code section. Examples of public disturbance include excessive burnouts, repeated revving of engine, loud music, and improper use of a horn.

charges.title: Speeding
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Speeding.

degrees.label: Speeding 1-10
degrees.description: A person who drives up to 10 mph over the posted speed limit is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Speeding 11-25
degrees.description: A person who drives greater than 11 mph but less than 25 mph over the posted speed limit is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Speeding 26-39
degrees.description: A person who drives greater than 26 mph but less than 39 mph over the posted speed limit is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Reckless Speeding (40+)
degrees.description: A person who drives more than 40 mph over the posted speed limit is guilty of reckless speeding under this code section. This crime is a misdemeanor as defined, but does not inherently carry any jail penalty.

charges.title: Operator Licensing and Presentation
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Operator Licensing and Presentation.

degrees.label: Unlicensed Operation of Vehicle
degrees.description: A person who operates a motor vehicle on a public roadway without the proper licenses for that vehicle class is guilty under this section.

degrees.label: Failing to Present a Driver's License
degrees.sourceTitle: Failing to Present a Driver's License
degrees.description: A person who operates a motor vehicle on a public roadway who fails to display upon the request of an officer a valid driver's license is guilty under this section.

degrees.label: Piloting without Proper Licensing
degrees.description: A person who operates an aircraft without the appropriate licenses is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Traffic Movement and Parking Violations
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Traffic Movement and Parking Violations.

degrees.label: Illegal U-Turn
degrees.description: A person who turns around on a roadway where the markings do not permit it is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Illegal Passing
degrees.description: A person who passes traffic illegally, unsafely, or where the markings do not permit it is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Failure to Maintain Lane
degrees.description: A person who crosses over lane markings where they do not permit passing, or when passing in an unsafe manner, is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Illegal Turn
degrees.description: A person who makes a turn from the wrong lane, or where signs disallow it, is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Unauthorized Parking
degrees.description: A person who parks their vehicle while blocking the roadway, on a red curb, on a pedestrian walkway, or in a marked no parking zone is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Hit and Run
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Hit and Run.

degrees.label: Hit and Run
degrees.description: A person who is involved in a motor vehicle accident, and does not stop to exchange license or insurance information is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Hunting Violations
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Hunting Violations.

degrees.label: Hunting in Restricted Areas
degrees.description: A person who hunts for game outside of allocated hunting areas is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Unlicensed Hunting
degrees.description: A person who hunts for game without the appropriate license is guilty under this code section.

degrees.label: Hunting with a Non-Hunting Weapon
degrees.description: A person who hunts game with a weapon that is not a legal licensed hunting weapon is guilty under this code section. Legal licensed hunting weapons are defined as weapons that are sold direct-to-consumer by official hunting stores, with weapons being automatically registered to the individual who purchases the weapon.

degrees.label: Hunting outside of hunting hours
degrees.description: A person who hunts game outside of the legal hours for hunting in that area is guilty under this code section. Legal hunting hours are from dawn to dusk in all legal hunting zones.

degrees.label: Overhunting
degrees.description: A person who hunts over the amount allowed in a given hunting area is guilty under this code section. The current amount legally allowed by a single individual is defined as 200 kg of combined animal meat and skins, fur, tusks, or other animal by-products.

degrees.label: Animal Poaching
degrees.description: A person who hunts for an endangered or protected species in the state of San Andreas is guilty under this code section. Current endangered and protected species include the following: Mountain Lions, Bears, Seagulls, and Capybara.

charges.title: Animal Cruelty
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Animal Cruelty.

degrees.label: Animal Cruelty
degrees.description: A person who causes harm to an animal with malicious intent, with no cause for self defence, is guilty under this code section.

charges.title: Fishing Violations
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Fishing Violations.

degrees.label: Fishing in an Unauthorized Zone
degrees.description: Unless otherwise specified, fishing for sport and game may be conducted on public waterways in the State of Los Santos. The following areas are prohibited fishing grounds: Zancudo River, Lago Zancudo Wetlands, Land Act Reservoir, and the Port of Los Santos

degrees.label: Illegal Fishing
degrees.description: Illegal fishing is defined as fishing using illegal hooks or possessing, transporting, or selling fish of prohibited lengths or species. Species that are prohibited from possession include: whales, sharks, arapaima, giant snakehead, goliath tigerfish, devil rays, kraken, megalodon, giant coelacanth, and golden fish. These fish, however, may be caught for sport and then immediately released. The minimum and maximum lengths for fish that may be caught, transported and sold are as follows (in inches): Salmon |30 - 40|, Trout |20 - 30|, Bass |20 - 30|, Catfish |18 - 30|, Perch |5 - 10|, Pike |22 -38 in|, Carp |15 - 30|, Bluegill |7 - 14 in|, Cod |30 - 45|, Herring |7 - 13|, Walleye |26 - 38|, Bream |12 - 22|, Zander |22 - 38|, Sturgeon |100 - 130|, Swordfish |65 - 95|, Tuna |50 - 75|, Muskellunge |32 - 48|, Tarpon |75 - 110|, Giant Catfish |85 - 115|. Individuals found to be illegally possessing, transporting, or selling these species are subject to confiscation of the fish and subject to the penalties outlined herein.

degrees.label: Overfishing
degrees.description: Daily catch limit is 30 fish per person per day. Violations of the catch limit are punishable by up to $600 per fish above the allotted amount. All fishing vessels and subjects who are engaged in fishing are subject to random inspections by Fish & Wildlife Officers for the stated purpose of monitoring fishing equipment, fish length, and all other duties.

charges.title: Police Working Dog Murder
charges.description: Grouped charge family for Police Working Dog Murder.

degrees.label: Murder of a Police Working Dog
degrees.sourceTitle: Murder of a Police Working Dog
degrees.description: A person who commits the intentional killing of a police working dog, while in the execution of their duties, in a way that is willful, deliberate and premeditated is guilty under this code section.

liabilityOptions.label: Attempted Murder of a Police Working Dog
liabilityOptions.sourceTitle: Attempted Murder of a Police Working Dog

liabilityOptions.label: Accessory to the Murder of a Police Working Dog
liabilityOptions.sourceTitle: Accessory to the Murder of a Police Working Dog
